title: Verify hotel search by popular location (minimum 10 hotel cards must appear)
reason: agoda.com opened and a search for the popular destination Bangkok loaded a results page displaying 12 hotel cards, exceeding the minimum requirement of 10.

title: Verifying sorting the results based on price
reason: After sorting by 'Lowest price' the results were displayed lowest-price-first (ascending: USD 6, then USD 7), which contradicts the expected result requiring the highest price first in descending order.

bug_details.title: Sorting by 'Lowest price' shows results lowest-first ascending instead of highest-first descending
bug_details.description: On the Agoda Bangkok hotel search results page, selecting the 'Lowest price' sort option (URL changes to sort=priceLowToHigh) arranges the hotel cards in ascending order with the cheapest property at the top (observed nightly prices from top: USD 6, then USD 7). The test case's expected result requires the results to be shown with the highest price first and then descending, which never occurs when sorting by lowest price.
steps_to_reproduce: Search for the destination 'Bangkok' and open the results page
Open the 'Sort by' control and select 'Lowest price'
Observe the order of the displayed hotel prices from top to bottom
bug_details.expected_result: The results are shown with the highest price first and then in descending order.
bug_details.actual_result: The results are shown with the lowest price first in ascending order (e.g., USD 6, then USD 7), not highest-price-first descending.
